purpose: Summarize why the notification digest repo demonstrates continuity across agent sessions.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.content: Summarize why the notification digest repo demonstrates continuity across agent sessions.

sections.title: Project
sections.content: agent-session-continuity: Cognitive repo for demonstrating multi-session agent continuity

sections.title: Goals
sections.content: - [Active] Ship a notification digest that an agent can continue across sessions: Keep the feature state explicit enough that a later session can resume and close it without relying on chat history.

sections.title: Tasks
sections.content:
- [Done] Define the first digest output contract: Create the first pass of the digest around unread notifications and leave the next missing behavior explicit.
- [Done] Lock the final behavior with executable tests: Close the feature with repeatable evidence so the next session does not reopen solved ground.
- [Done] Resume later and add quiet-hours filtering: Continue from the recorded state instead of chat memory and finish the remaining digest behavior.

sections.title: Hypotheses
sections.content:
- [Supported] (90%) A later agent session can continue correctly if the unfinished behavior is recorded explicitly as task state, hypothesis, and evidence instead of being left in chat.
- [Supported] (89%) Executable evidence is required to preserve session continuity because later sessions should trust the repository state instead of re-evaluating finished behavior from scratch.
- [Supported] (87%) Quiet-hours filtering should exclude unread notifications that arrive inside the blocked time window while keeping daytime unread items in the digest.

sections.title: Decisions
sections.content:
- [Accepted] Treat quiet-hours filtering as the explicit continuation point: The first session should stop after defining the digest contract and leave the next missing behavior visible in CTX.
- [Accepted] Resume from recorded state instead of restating the feature in chat: The session transition worked because the pending behavior was already explicit in CTX and the next step was discoverable without conversational reconstruction.
- [Accepted] Keep quiet-hours filtering in the digest builder: The implementation keeps the unread digest contract from session 1 and adds the deferred filtering rule in a targeted way.
- [Accepted] Close the digest feature with executable state, not conversational memory: The feature is only ready for future agent continuity once behavior and tests are both recorded in CTX-backed evidence.

sections.title: Evidence
sections.content:
- [Experiment] (99%) Executable tests confirm unread selection and quiet-hours suppression: The digest test suite verifies unread notification selection and confirms that notifications inside quiet hours are excluded while daytime notifications remain visible.
- [Experiment] (97%) Regression tests anchor the final feature state for later sessions: The passing test suite gives the next session a concrete artifact that the digest behavior is already correct and does not need to be rediscovered.
- [Document] (93%) Session 2 resumes from explicit continuation notes: The second session note resumes from the recorded pending work and focuses directly on quiet-hours filtering instead of rediscovering the feature scope.
- [Document] (92%) Session 1 leaves quiet-hours as explicit unfinished work: The first session note records that the digest shape is defined and quiet-hours filtering remains intentionally pending for the next session.
- [Observation] (90%) Digest builder implements quiet-hours filtering: The digest builder now excludes unread notifications that fall inside the configured quiet-hours window.

sections.title: Conclusions
sections.content: - [Accepted] The notification digest feature now demonstrates multi-session continuity: session 1 left explicit pending work, session 2 resumed from CTX, implemented quiet-hours filtering, and closed the feature with executable evidence.
